title: gardenia subcarinata
intro: Gardenia subcarinata, also known as the "corky gardenia," is a species of flowering plant in the genus Gardenia, native to Madagascar. The plant's flowers and leaves are used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to their rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Gardenia subcarinata have been associated with anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing pain, improving digestion, and protecting against infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be administered orally or topically to harness their therapeutic properties.
